text.title: azure-devops-boards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-devops-boards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: WIQL WAS queries on large issue sets for historical analysis.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WAS queries on transition history time out above approximately 10,000 issues. Use Analytics WorkItemRevisions entity via OData for historical state analysis.

failure-mode.description: Writing Markdown in ADO description or comment fields from an agent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ADO description accepts HTML, not Markdown. Agent-written Markdown displays as literal syntax or empty content. Convert to HTML using pandoc or a sanitizer before writing.

failure-mode.description: Bulk-creating more than 50 work items per batch in parallel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ADO throttles bulk creation; unthrottled parallel batches trigger 429s. Chunk in batches of 50 with exponential backoff and check for the Retry-After header.

failure-mode.description: WIQL queries with dropped trailing brackets or misquoted field names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WIQL is whitespace-sensitive and returns empty results (200 OK, zero items) for malformed queries. Always validate WIQL syntax against a known-good schema before executing.

failure-mode.description: Using display names instead of identity SIDs in @mention comments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ADO @mentions require the user identity SID, not the display name. Display names are non-unique and produce invalid mentions. Resolve via the Identities API first.
